race.name: Pirkan Hiihto
race.tagline: Ninety kilometers across the Finnish lakeland — a silent, frozen marathon through the country that treats cross-country skiing as a birthright.

vitals.aid_stations: 12 service points with warm drinks, soup, and energy food

climate.description: Finland's interior delivers consistently cold, stable February conditions. Temperatures range from -20°C to -5°C. The flat, forested terrain provides some wind shelter but frozen lake crossings expose skiers. Snow depth is typically excellent — Finland's continental climate produces dry, fast powder.
challenges: Sustained cold over 90km demands layering strategy
Flat terrain means constant self-pacing with no downhill recovery
Wind exposure on frozen lake sections
Fueling across 12 service points requires nutrition planning

course.primary: Point-to-point through the Finnish lakeland from Niinisalo to Tampere, predominantly flat terrain through boreal forest and across frozen lakes. The 90km distance makes this a true ultra-marathon on skis.
course.surface: Machine-groomed tracks through forest and frozen lake terrain
course.grooming: Finnish standard grooming, well-maintained with 12 service points
features: 90km main distance — one of the longest Worldloppet races
12 fully-stocked service points along the course
Finishes in Tampere, Finland's third-largest city
Classic Finnish lakeland landscape — forest, lakes, silence
Both classic and skate techniques available

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: The 90km distance and perfect snow reliability anchor the score. Finland's deep skiing culture delivers strong organization and community. Flat terrain and modest scenery prevent a higher ceiling.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 eligible for 1-tier promotion, already at T2

history.summary: Founded in 1955, Pirkan Hiihto is Finland's premier marathon ski race, covering 90 kilometers from the garrison town of Niinisalo to the city of Tampere. The race was established to promote long-distance skiing in the Pirkanmaa region and has grown to approximately 3,000 participants. With 12 service points, it is one of the best-supported marathon courses in Nordic skiing. The event embodies Finland's deep cultural relationship with cross-country skiing.
notable_facts: One of the longest races in the Worldloppet series at 90km
12 service points — among the most supported courses in marathon skiing
Finland's flagship marathon ski race since 1955
The course from Niinisalo to Tampere crosses classic Finnish lakeland
Strong domestic participation from Finland's large recreational skiing community
